steps.title: Identify the collision of two independent clauses
steps.content: The sentence contains an independent clause ending with “SN 2005ap was a supernova (the explosion of a massive star),” followed by “the transient yet powerful blast propelled particles and debris...” — a second independent clause requiring proper separation.

steps.title: Apply the period rule for sentence boundaries
steps.content:
Two independent clauses must be separated by a period, semicolon, or coordinating conjunction + comma.

Choice B uses a period after “star)” to create two sentences: the first defines the supernova, and the second begins “Occurring in the constellation Coma Berenices, 4.7 billion light-years from Earth, the transient yet powerful blast propelled...” where the participial phrase properly introduces the main clause.

The other three choices either lack punctuation after the parenthesis (A, D) or create a comma splice (C).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
